content.question:
Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

1. I don't think his statement bears ___ this case.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

2. You can't change your mind now. It's too late to back ___ the deal.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

3. The plant manager answers directly ___ the head of the company.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

4. I was prepared to back ___ her story because I knew it was the truth.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

5. The baby has been acting ___ all day. I think she must be teething.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

6. The murderer will answer ___ his crimes in the highest court in the land.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

7. Faced with such formidable opposition to his plans, he had no choice but to back ___ .

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

8. The teacher accused him of answering her ___ when he attempted to explain what he had been doing.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

9. If you can bear ___ me a little longer, I'll tell you the rest of the story.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

10. After a violent attack in the press on their welfare policies, the Conservatives answered ___ with a statement of their reforms since taking office.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

11. The cinema which was adjacent ___ the bank was damaged when a bomb exploded in the bank.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

12. What you're saying amounts ___ blackmail.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

13. Mr Parker was arrested ___ driving while under the influence of alcohol.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

14. She finds it difficult adjusting ___ the climate.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

15. Your calculations do not accord ___ mine.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

16. She was very appreciative ___ all the support she got from her friends.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

17. I was totally abashed ___ his rude manner.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

18. His abstinence ___ alcohol lasted only two months.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

19. I have an aversion ___ spiders.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

20. The child showed no animosity ___ her new stepbrother.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

21. The recommendations are based ___ a recent report by the Home Office.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

22. My little sister still believes ___ Father Christmas.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

23. Beware ___ strong currents when swimming in this area.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

24. I bumped ___ an old school friend in town last week.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

25. The tourists bartered ___ their souvenirs at the local market.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

26. The children arrived at the fair and made a beeline ___ the ghost train.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

27. There's a ban ___ using hose-pipes because of the drought.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

28. The cat basked ___ the warm sunshine.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

29. He continually boasts ___ his fantastic job.

Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition or adverb.

30. The man begged his wife ___ forgiveness.
